title: Dedicated tenant batching-tier override not taking effect (route policy appears to win over tenant override)
preview:
Issue summary
- Dedicated customer reports that their tenant-level override for batching SLO tier (fast) is not being applied; effective behavior remains standard.
- Customer expectation (and internal design): customer override > route policy > tier default.
- Observed: route policy appears to override tenant settin...
text:
Issue summary
- Dedicated customer reports that their tenant-level override for batching SLO tier (fast) is not being applied; effective behavior remains standard.
- Customer expectation (and internal design): customer override > route policy > tier default.
- Observed: route policy appears to override tenant setting (or tenant setting is ignored).

Customer impact
- Customer is attempting to force fast tier on a latency-sensitive streaming endpoint.
- They are seeing increased queueing and higher p99 compared to pre-tiered-defaults behavior.
- Impact limited to a subset of routes on their Dedicated cluster, but blocks their rollout of tiered defaults.

Environment
- Dedicated cluster: northlake-dedicated-03
- Region: us-east
- Runtime train: 1.22.0 (feature flag enabled for tiered defaults)
- Control plane: 2026.04.08 build (policy evaluation service)

What the customer configured
1)...
links: Customer screenshots (Console override + route policy) attached in Jira (redacted).
Staging repro trace: decision_path shows route policy applied after tenant override (pre-fix).

title: SEV-2: eu-west latency spike after runtime build 2026.03.18.0 (kernel selection)
preview:
Issue summary: p95 latency regression for long-context traffic in eu-west after runtime build 2026.03.18.0.

Impact: SLO burn for chat long-context bucket; customers saw slower responses but low error rate.

Environment: prod eu-west, A100 pools.

Investigation notes: Suspected kernel selection heuristic change; per...
text:
Issue summary: p95 latency regression for long-context traffic in eu-west after runtime build 2026.03.18.0.

Impact: SLO burn for chat long-context bucket; customers saw slower responses but low error rate.

Environment: prod eu-west, A100 pools.

Investigation notes: Suspected kernel selection heuristic change; perf-canary shows regression aligned with build.

Mitigation: Disable heuristic via feature flag; force kernel to prior stable variant.

title: Suspicious mass auth attempts after internal "job application" form; possible credential harvest impacting service accounts
preview: Summary: On 2026-03-13 09:42 UTC the security monitoring alerting pipeline flagged a clustered spike of failed and then some successful token exchanges from several service accounts following a widely-circulated internal "job application" Google Form. The pattern suggests credentials may have been harvested via the ...
text:
Summary: On 2026-03-13 09:42 UTC the security monitoring alerting pipeline flagged a clustered spike of failed and then some successful token exchanges from several service accounts following a widely-circulated internal "job application" Google Form. The pattern suggests credentials may have been harvested via the form and subsequently used to attempt programmatic access.

Impact: Multiple service-account API tokens and a small number of CI systems observed anomalous auth activity. No confirmed data exfiltration detected so far. Some internal CI jobs failed due to token rotation triggered after suspicious logins. Customer-facing services are not known to be affected.

Detection: Real-time SIEM rules (auth-anomaly-001) and the anomaly detector for token-exchange rate detected a burst between 09:25–09:47 UTC with (>120) failed refreshes and 7 successful refreshes originating from 3 pub...

preview:
Issue summary:
We need to onboard vendor 'LabelWave' for a 6-week labeling pilot. The vendor requires: SCIM provisioning for ephemeral contractor accounts, BYO-KMS support for storing exported label artifacts, and a single invoice aligned to our FY Q3 renewal window.

- Research & Data team cannot start the ...
text:
Issue summary:
We need to onboard vendor 'LabelWave' for a 6-week labeling pilot. The vendor requires: SCIM provisioning for ephemeral contractor accounts, BYO-KMS support for storing exported label artifacts, and a single invoice aligned to our FY Q3 renewal window.

Impact:
- Research & Data team cannot start the pilot until seat provisioning is available.
- Contractors currently require manual invites which increases onboarding time by ~2 days per contractor and creates security auditing gaps.
- If renewal dates are not aligned, we will incur multiple pro-rated invoices and extra procurement overhead.

Request / Acceptance criteria:
1) Create a centralized ephemeral license pool (50 seats) that can be handed out to contractors and reclaimed automatically via SCIM deprovisioning.
2) Configure SCIM integration with LabelWave and map attributes: email, manager, cost_center tag.
3) Val...

title: Restricted forensic sample extract for privacy-incident reproduction
preview:
Issue summary:
A customer reported a potential PII leak in generated outputs from an application using redwood/longform-v1. We need a narrowly scoped forensic sample extract from production inference logs to reproduce the issue and validate whether PII appeared in model outputs.

Justification:
- Reproduction requir...
text:
Issue summary:
A customer reported a potential PII leak in generated outputs from an application using redwood/longform-v1. We need a narrowly scoped forensic sample extract from production inference logs to reproduce the issue and validate whether PII appeared in model outputs.

Justification:
- Reproduction required to determine root cause (model prompt leakage vs. prompt content).
- Small, time-boxed sample minimizes exposure.

Scope of data requested:
- Time window: 2026-03-11 00:00:00 to 2026-03-11 23:59:59 (UTC)
- Up to 200 inference records matching the customer tenant and the reported conversation hashes.
- Include fields: request_id, customer_tenant_id (hashed), request_prompt (redacted), response_text (original and redacted copy), model_version, routing_info, timestamp, and system metadata (instance id, region).
- Explicit exclusion: do NOT include raw user identifiers or cu...

title: Provisioning/Repair: Fleet power policy rollout locks CPU at low P-state causing Windows container start failures
preview:
Issue summary:
A recent Intune-driven corporate power policy rollout applied to a subset of developer and infra workstations appears to be forcing minimum processor states and other AC power parameters that lock CPU performance at the lowest P-state on some systems. Affected machines show sustained ~400 MHz CPU freq...
text:
Issue summary:
A recent Intune-driven corporate power policy rollout applied to a subset of developer and infra workstations appears to be forcing minimum processor states and other AC power parameters that lock CPU performance at the lowest P-state on some systems. Affected machines show sustained ~400 MHz CPU frequency on AC power after provisioning/re-imaging which causes Windows containers (Docker Desktop and Windows Server containers used for local dev) to fail health checks and refuse to start reliably.

Impact:
- Several internal engineers and platform developers cannot start local containers; CI jobs that rely on Windows container hosts fail on self-hosted runners on affected machines.
- Issue observed on ~12 confirmed devices in the us-west fleet; likely more unreported.
- Productivity impact (blocked local dev, intermittent CI failures). No customer-facing outage.

Environme...

title: Temporary telemetry query privilege for data-minimization audit
preview:
Issue summary:
Request from Compliance (Privacy) to run a constrained set of telemetry queries against production request logs to confirm that new data-minimization rules have reduced retention of user identifiers in prefix caches. This is not an export request; Compliance needs aggregated metrics and a small number...
text:
Issue summary:
Request from Compliance (Privacy) to run a constrained set of telemetry queries against production request logs to confirm that new data-minimization rules have reduced retention of user identifiers in prefix caches. This is not an export request; Compliance needs aggregated metrics and a small number of sampled rows to validate masking rules.

Background:
- New data-minimization policy shipped 2026-03-05 adjusted KV cache retention and redaction on session keys.
- Compliance requires a short verification window to confirm no PII leakage in the last 7 days of production telemetry.

Impact / Risk:
- High sensitivity: production logs contain request metadata and potentially user-provided content.
- Goal is to avoid broad access to raw payloads and ensure least-privilege.

Requested access:
- JIT (just-in-time) query privilege for Compliance engineer: Duration 4 hours, sin...

title: Windows 11 roaming + adapter power-save causing WireGuard route ghosting and DNS failures on corporate Wi‑Fi
preview:
Issue summary:
Windows 11 laptops intermittently lose connectivity to internal services after roaming between APs. The WireGuard interface remains up but the system installs a stale 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing at the local wifi gateway (metric low), effectively blackholing outbound traffic for processes bound to the wg...
text:
Issue summary:
Windows 11 laptops intermittently lose connectivity to internal services after roaming between APs. The WireGuard interface remains up but the system installs a stale 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing at the local wifi gateway (metric low), effectively blackholing outbound traffic for processes bound to the wg interface. DNS queries either fail or go to the wrong resolver.

Impact:
- ~8 active users in the SF office reporting repeated failures during heavy roaming (moving between floors / boardroom).
- Symptoms: applications time out (internal dashboards, git pushes), developer shells lose connections, Slack remains connected but messages to internal services fail.

Environment:
- OS: Windows 11 22H2 (build 22631.x)
- Wireless chipset: Intel AX211 / AX201 (driver 22.50.1 reported on affected machines)
- VPN: WireGuard v0.7.6 (enterprise config via enterprise-agent)
- Zero-trust/...

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference front...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports large p99/p50 throughput collapse during the daily autoscale window (09:00-09:12 UTC) for their dedicated pool. Impact: production request latency p99 jumped from ~700ms to 6–12s, and sustained token throughput dropped by ~55% for ~8 minutes.

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference frontends experienced long tail latencies and 5xx spikes (mostly 502/504).
- Several requests timed out causing downstream job failures in ScribeAI pipelines.
- Business impact: queued jobs and delayed processing for ~350k inference tokens.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool id: dp-78a4 (ScribeAI reserved cluster)
- Region: us-east-1
- Serving runtime version: v2.18.3
- Autoscaler version: v1.9.1
- Model variants: redwood/gpt-4o-mini-v1 pinned at 2026-03-10

Steps to reproduce (customer provided):

title: Concurrent client retries split transaction boundary causing duplicate tool executions across Python and Go SDKs
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports duplicated external side effects (webhook POST to order processing) when their app issues concurrent retries from both Python (2.4.1) and Go (1.7.0) SDK clients in a short window. Impact: Duplicate orders reaching downstream systems, billing exposure, and customer trust concern. Obser...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports duplicated external side effects (webhook POST to order processing) when their app issues concurrent retries from both Python (2.4.1) and Go (1.7.0) SDK clients in a short window. Impact: Duplicate orders reaching downstream systems, billing exposure, and customer trust concern. Observed behavior: Under burst failure conditions the orchestrator appears to split what the client treats as a single transactional interaction into two distinct server-side request groups, and two tool invocations are delivered to the downstream webhook. Expected behavior: A single external tool call (webhook) per logical user action even if the client retries locally; SDK/platform should provide clear idempotency guarantee guidance and mechanisms to prevent duplicate side-effects.

title: TCP timestamp stripping by corporate firewall causes intermittent gRPC reconnects and degraded streaming in private deployment
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers on a private VPC deployment intermittently observe gRPC stream resets and frequent reconnects when sending long-lived streaming inference requests. Impact: streaming clients experience token duplication, reconnect churn, and increased tail latency, causing transient user-facing failures duri...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers on a private VPC deployment intermittently observe gRPC stream resets and frequent reconnects when sending long-lived streaming inference requests. Impact: streaming clients experience token duplication, reconnect churn, and increased tail latency, causing transient user-facing failures during payments reconciliation jobs.

Impact:
- Multiple customers reported reconnect storms during peak batch inference windows (FinNova observed ~8% request failures, clients retried and duplicated outputs).
- Tail latency increases from 120ms median to >1.5s p95 for streaming responses.

Environment:
- Deployment: Private (customer-managed VPC peering + egress through corporate firewall)
- Region: us-east-1 (customer-routed egress)
- Redwood runtime: serving-runtime v2026.02.10
- OBS: TLS stays up; application-level gRPC shows RST and transient EOFs.

title: Intermittent 5xx spike from prefetch-circuit coupling causing memory pressure and runtime restart loop
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer (ChorusAI) reported a sudden elevation in 5xx responses on streaming and short-request routes starting 2026-03-13 14:10 UTC. Errors manifest as 502/503 from API gateway, and upstream runtimes in the customers dedicated pool repeatedly restart. Impact: customer reports degraded UX (failed chat...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer (ChorusAI) reported a sudden elevation in 5xx responses on streaming and short-request routes starting 2026-03-13 14:10 UTC. Errors manifest as 502/503 from API gateway, and upstream runtimes in the customers dedicated pool repeatedly restart. Impact: customer reports degraded UX (failed chat completions) and elevated token-level retries.

Impact:
- ~35% error rate on the affected route for the tenant during the peak window.
- Multiple upstream runtime processes observed OOM/restarts.
- Customer requests are predominantly small/prefix-heavy (streaming chat) mixed with a small embedding upload job.

Environment:
- Dedicated tenant cluster in us-east-1, production.
- Autoscaler: dedicated-gpu-pool-v3.

Steps to reproduce (observed in production):
1. Tenant performs a short burst of streaming chat traffic (100-200 RPS) concurrently with a 50k-row embedding upload ...

title: Unexpected 5xx surge from accept-queue / prefetch resonance causing runtime preemptions
preview:
Issue summary:
A sustained elevation in 5xx responses originating at the API gateway was observed between 2026-03-12 09:05 UTC and ongoing. Root cause appears tied to an interaction between accept-queue growth and aggressive prefetch / KV warmup on dedicated runtimes, which amplifies upstream latency and triggers GP...
text:
Issue summary:
A sustained elevation in 5xx responses originating at the API gateway was observed between 2026-03-12 09:05 UTC and ongoing. Root cause appears tied to an interaction between accept-queue growth and aggressive prefetch / KV warmup on dedicated runtimes, which amplifies upstream latency and triggers GPU preemption (watchdog) on some ephemeral workers.

Impact:
- Customer: VertexDocs (dedicated) reported ~35% failed requests for chat and embedding routes during the window.
- User-facing: increased 5xx and 503/502 responses, elevated tail latency on chat-generation endpoints, degraded throughput for concurrent embedding batches.
- Business: Affected production traffic across a single us-east shard; no billing anomalies reported.

Environment:
- Deployment: Dedicated pool (us-east) serving VertexDocs tenant (dedicated).
- Runtime: inference-runtime v2.14.1 with kernel selec...

preview: Issue summary: LuminaHealth began reporting elevated 5xx rates (503/502) and streaming resets starting ~2026-03-11 14:10 PDT after enabling a high-throughput streaming integration. Impact: Several service endpoints returned sustained 5xx for ~90 minutes, degrading real-time app behavior and triggering customer alert...
text: Issue summary: LuminaHealth began reporting elevated 5xx rates (503/502) and streaming resets starting ~2026-03-11 14:10 PDT after enabling a high-throughput streaming integration. Impact: Several service endpoints returned sustained 5xx for ~90 minutes, degrading real-time app behavior and triggering customer alerts. Environment: prod-us-west dedicated tenant on Redwood Dedicated. Immediate customer impact: realtime chat sessions dropping, message retries, increased latency.

title: Steady 429s for mixed file-upload + function-calling streams after burst-window config change
preview:

Customer LumenDocs is seeing sustained 429 responses for a subset of requests after we applied a change to their dedicated pool burst window (reduced from 10s to 4s). The requests are a mix of file uploads (multipart POST -> upstream file store) followed immediately by function-calling streams that op...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer LumenDocs is seeing sustained 429 responses for a subset of requests after we applied a change to their dedicated pool burst window (reduced from 10s to 4s). The requests are a mix of file uploads (multipart POST -> upstream file store) followed immediately by function-calling streams that open a streaming websocket for model inference.

Impact:
- ~12% of user sessions experience immediate 429s when the client uploads a file and triggers a function-calling stream within 2s.
- Customer reports degraded UX for their document ingestion + extract pipeline.
- Affects one dedicated pool (LumenDocs-prod-us-west).

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated pool: lumen-prod-pool-7
- Region: us-west (single AZ)
- Client SDK: JS v2.5.1 (multiplexed websocket + fallback to http), custom upload proxy in front of SDK
- Quota config: per-tenant token bucket + short burst window (4s) a...

title: Burst 502/503s from GPU worker thread starvation during large-embedding job with keepalive churn
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers observe short bursts (~30s-2m) of 502/503 responses originating at the API gateway when a large embedding job (approx 1.2M tokens across many small requests) runs concurrently with normal traffic. Rate of errors correlates with embedding job spikes and periods of many short-lived streaming s...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers observe short bursts (~30s-2m) of 502/503 responses originating at the API gateway when a large embedding job (approx 1.2M tokens across many small requests) runs concurrently with normal traffic. Rate of errors correlates with embedding job spikes and periods of many short-lived streaming sessions (keepalive churn).

Impact:
- LuminaSearch (dedicated) reported 15-30% request error-rate increase during bursts, impacting live search indexing.
- Errors are transient but repeatable with similar workload pattern; downstream retries amplify load.

Environment:
- Prod, us-east, Dedicated GPU pool (A100s), serving-runtime v2026.02.7, nvml driver v470.x, API gateway v1.14.3

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Start large embedding job: 12k concurrent small embedding requests batched by customer SDK into many parallel connections.
2) Introduce a stream of short-lived stre...

title: Request: sampled audit correlation map and SIEM onboarding checklist for multi-tenant activity logs
preview: Issue summary: Customer requests a compact, sampled audit correlation map plus a SIEM onboarding checklist for Fincrest Bank's pilot integration. Impact: They need a deterministic sampled export to validate cross-system joins (user_id->session_id->request_id) for compliance and fraud analytics without shipping full-...
text: Issue summary: Customer requests a compact, sampled audit correlation map plus a SIEM onboarding checklist for Fincrest Bank's pilot integration. Impact: They need a deterministic sampled export to validate cross-system joins (user_id->session_id->request_id) for compliance and fraud analytics without shipping full-volume logs. Goal is to provide example payloads, recommended ECS/EK mappings, HMAC manifest approach for sample batches, and a checklist for configuration in Splunk/Graylog/Elastic. Environment: Production tenant for Fincrest (multi-tenant; retention 90 days; VPC egress allowed to their ingestion endpoints in us-east).

title: Microburst pipelining causing GPU starvation and autoscaler stall in dedicated pool
preview: Issue summary: ClearView reported a sharp degradation in p50/p99 latency and a throughput collapse for routes pinned to their dedicated pool. The behavior coincided with a burst of many short-lived streaming inference requests from the customer's application. Impact: multiple ClearView routes experienced 5-10x p99 l...
text: Issue summary: ClearView reported a sharp degradation in p50/p99 latency and a throughput collapse for routes pinned to their dedicated pool. The behavior coincided with a burst of many short-lived streaming inference requests from the customer's application. Impact: multiple ClearView routes experienced 5-10x p99 latency increases for ~20 minutes during business hours; customer-facing degradation triggered a support SLA escalation. Timeline: 2026-03-12 09:02 UTC — first alert from customer; 09:05 — support triaged and opened ticket; 09:20 — SRE observed sustained GPU queue depth and slot utilization anomalies; 09:45 — applied temporary mitigation (reserve slots), 10:10 — mitigation restored baseline for ClearView. Observed behavior: - High slot fragmentation: dozens of slots occupied briefly by short streaming sessions, preventing efficient batching. - Autoscaler did not scale up des...

title: Dedicated pool throughput drop during quiet windows; transient GPU thinness traced to sidecar burst spike

text:
Issue summary:
- Customer (QuillSync) reports periodic dedicated-pool throughput drop during their midday quiet windows (low traffic), recurring over 3 days. Affected routes are pinned to dedicated pool in us-west-2.
Impact:
- P50/P95 throughput decreased by ~30% for 2-6 minute windows; increased request queueing and observed fallback to shared tier for a subset of requests. Customer-facing latency spikes and elevated 429/504 retries.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Traffic profile: send steady low-rate traffic mimicking QuillSync background sync (10-15 concurrent short streams).
2) Wait for scheduled telemetry window (~midday, customer local timezone).
3) Observe allocator/gpu attach logs and autoscaler events for pool quillsync-prod-pool-1.

title: Synchronous connector insertion causes intermittent large E2E latency spikes and request timeouts
preview: Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 22:00 UTC NovaRetail is observing intermittent generation requests hitting 5–20s tail latencies and occasional 504s when the model is asked to call external connectors. Impact: Production storefront chat widgets are timing out for a subset of calls that trigger the customer's connecto...
text: Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 22:00 UTC NovaRetail is observing intermittent generation requests hitting 5–20s tail latencies and occasional 504s when the model is asked to call external connectors. Impact: Production storefront chat widgets are timing out for a subset of calls that trigger the customer's connector workflow (~3–8% of sessions). This is causing failed recommendations and a visible product regression. Environment: Hosted Redwood API, customer on enterprise plan with traffic routed through a customer-configured connector that performs synchronous validation and an additional metadata lookup before the model generates the final text. This connector was recently enabled in a canary rollout.

title: Dedicated capacity uneven shard affinity causing GPU starvation and autoscaler oscillation during off-peak window
preview: Issue summary: NovusHealth observed an intermittent but sustained p50/p95 throughput degradation in their dedicated pool during a nightly off-peak window (approx 02:00-04:30 UTC). Requests queue and remain pending for several seconds despite spare GPU capacity reported across the cluster. Impact: customer-facing lat...
text:
Issue summary: NovusHealth observed an intermittent but sustained p50/p95 throughput degradation in their dedicated pool during a nightly off-peak window (approx 02:00-04:30 UTC). Requests queue and remain pending for several seconds despite spare GPU capacity reported across the cluster. Impact: customer-facing latency regressions and elevated queue lengths for a production model pinned to redwood-open-7b-v2. Environment: Dedicated reserved pool (pool-id: p-7461), region us-west-2, model pinned to redwood-open-7b-v2, customer uses small streaming requests (avg tokens/output small).

Observed behavior: autoscaler repeatedly scales down and back up during the window (oscillation) with short lived GPU attach/detach events. Several GPUs appear underutilized while others are saturated (high NVLink/PCIe stalls). Allocator logs show placement-group affinity enforcement that groups long-live...

title: Microphone fails to enumerate after BIOS flash + Secure Boot toggle during depot repair
preview:
Issue summary: After a standard depot BIOS flash followed by toggling Secure Boot to re-enable legacy imaging paths, attached device microphones (internal array) no longer enumerate in Windows. Observed on small subset of fleet following recent vendor BIOS (v1.18.4) rollout to depot units.

Impact: Affected users se...
text:
Issue summary: After a standard depot BIOS flash followed by toggling Secure Boot to re-enable legacy imaging paths, attached device microphones (internal array) no longer enumerate in Windows. Observed on small subset of fleet following recent vendor BIOS (v1.18.4) rollout to depot units.

Impact: Affected users see "No audio input device installed" in Settings and Device Manager; internal microphone missing from audio input list. External USB microphones still function. This blocks users from joining voice calls and impacts QA signoff for a subset of refurbished laptops. Approximately 7 confirmed units to date, all processed through the same depot workflow.

Environment: Depot repair/flash workflow, corp provisioning image (Win11-23H2-corp), Secure Boot toggled from Enabled -> Disabled -> Enabled during repair validation; BIOS version 1.18.4 on affected units.

title: Okta SSO login triggers captive-portal detection which drops VPN and causes authentication loop for remote Wi‑Fi users
preview: Issue summary: Several employees connecting from customer sites or hotel Wi‑Fi report that attempting to authenticate to internal apps via Okta SAML redirects triggers a captive‑portal flow. When users complete captive‑portal sign‑in, the VPN client (WireGuard-based corporate agent) detects the network change and te...
text:
Issue summary: Several employees connecting from customer sites or hotel Wi‑Fi report that attempting to authenticate to internal apps via Okta SAML redirects triggers a captive‑portal flow. When users complete captive‑portal sign‑in, the VPN client (WireGuard-based corporate agent) detects the network change and tears down the tunnel, which invalidates the SSO session and returns the user to the captive portal — creating a loop.

Impact: Multiple remote employees (mostly traveling sales and solutions engineers) are unable to access internal dashboards or Atlassian tools until they fully disconnect and re‑authenticate. Affected users temporarily lose access to internal services for ~10–30 minutes. No customer‑facing systems impacted; internal productivity degradation.

Environment: macOS and Windows corporate images, Redwood VPN agent v2.9.1 (WireGuard wrapper), Okta SAML SSO, typical...

title: Session linkage token mismatch blocks cross-account impersonation for support tooling
preview: Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC, support tooling (impersonation flows used by oncall engineers via the internal console and CLI) intermittently fail with a 'session token mismatch' error when initiating cross-account impersonation. Impact: Oncall engineers cannot assume customer-facing service accounts for...
text:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC, support tooling (impersonation flows used by oncall engineers via the internal console and CLI) intermittently fail with a 'session token mismatch' error when initiating cross-account impersonation. Impact: Oncall engineers cannot assume customer-facing service accounts for 5-30% of impersonation attempts, blocking urgent runbook actions and escalations. Environment: production us-east, identity-proxy v3.4.1, okta-device-trust enabled for privileged flows.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Login to console as admin (device trust approved)
2) Open 'Switch account / Impersonate' dialog and select external-tenant service-account
3) Click 'Assume role', or run 'rwd auth impersonate --account <tenant-id>' via CLI
4) Observe immediate failure in UI or CLI with error code 403/SESSION_LINK_MISMATCH and short correlation id in logs

Observed behavior: The ident...

title: Intermittent tail-drain timeouts for very large max_tokens / long-prefix streaming requests
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers are observing intermittent streaming disconnects and finalization timeouts when submitting requests with very large max_tokens (>=150k) and extended conversation history (many system/user messages). The generation runs for tens of seconds and then the client receives a 504 or streaming conne...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers are observing intermittent streaming disconnects and finalization timeouts when submitting requests with very large max_tokens (>=150k) and extended conversation history (many system/user messages). The generation runs for tens of seconds and then the client receives a 504 or streaming connection is closed without final chunk. This appears to correlate with sustained long-token drains and sudden KV cache thrashing on Dedicated pools.

Impact:
- Affected customer (NoteChat Inc., dedicated) reports ~10% of long-run streaming completions fail to finalize, causing truncated transcripts and user-visible errors.
- Business impact: production feature degradation for premium customers; customers are throttling long-context usage.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool (g4dn-like), prod-us-east, isolated tenant.
- Requests routed to serving runtime version v1.48.0 with bat...

title: Time-bounded evidence playback with per-entity obfuscation and canonical checksums
preview: Customer requests a time-bounded "playback" export for a legal discovery window (2026-02-10T00:00:00Z to 2026-02-15T23:59:59Z) that meets the following constraints: 1) Remove or obfuscate any customer-provided PII at entity level (names, SSNs, account numbers) while preserving conversation structure and action times...
text: Customer requests a time-bounded "playback" export for a legal discovery window (2026-02-10T00:00:00Z to 2026-02-15T23:59:59Z) that meets the following constraints: 1) Remove or obfuscate any customer-provided PII at entity level (names, SSNs, account numbers) while preserving conversation structure and action timestamps; 2) Provide a canonical, tamper-evident checksum manifest (SHA256 per payload + HMAC envelope signed with KMS) that proves ordering and integrity for chain-of-custody; 3) Include RFC3161 timestamp for the manifest; 4) Allow partial playback of a single tenant's events filtered by session_id and model_version; 5) Export must be available as a downloadable bundle and as a short-lived S3 presigned URL for third-party counsel. Customer explicitly requests that model-generated suggestions that echo PII are redacted but system-level metadata (route, model_version, request_i...

title: API Gateway 5xx amplification from connection-slab fragmentation and scheduler latency during tenant burst
preview: Issue summary: NoteStream reported a rapid increase in 5xx responses (502/503/504) beginning 2026-03-12 18:32 UTC during a scheduled bulk ingestion. Impact: production traffic for NoteStream (dedicated pool) experienced elevated error rates (~18% 5xx for 20 minutes) and increased tail latency causing customer thrott...
text:
Issue summary: NoteStream reported a rapid increase in 5xx responses (502/503/504) beginning 2026-03-12 18:32 UTC during a scheduled bulk ingestion. Impact: production traffic for NoteStream (dedicated pool) experienced elevated error rates (~18% 5xx for 20 minutes) and increased tail latency causing customer throttles and several client timeouts. Environment: prod, us-east, dedicated GPU pool.

Observed behavior: API Gateway began returning 502/503 in waves correlated with spikes in connection queue length and a burst of ephemeral worker churn. The serving runtime reported repeated short-lived OOMs and scheduler logs show latency spikes in task assignment. KV-cache metrics show increased shard contention and growth of pending prefetch queues.

Expected behavior: Traffic should remain within acceptable error floor (<0.1% 5xx) using the tenant's reserved capacity and fallback routing s...

title: Priority inversion in continuous batcher causing tenant-specific p99 latency spikes
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-12 ~09:10 UTC, NimbusPay reported sharp p95/p99 latency regressions for inference requests routed to their Dedicated pool in us-east. The regression appears correlated with recent continuous-batcher scheduler change that introduced fairness/priority weighting. Impact: p95 increased fr...
text:
Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-12 ~09:10 UTC, NimbusPay reported sharp p95/p99 latency regressions for inference requests routed to their Dedicated pool in us-east. The regression appears correlated with recent continuous-batcher scheduler change that introduced fairness/priority weighting. Impact: p95 increased from ~85ms baseline to ~720ms and p99 up to 4.2s for NimbusPay tenant traffic; production user-facing timeouts (504s) rose from 0.01% to 0.9% for the affected endpoints. Not all tenants are affected; behavior seems tenant-specific and workload-sensitive (mixed short and long sequences). Environment: prod (us-east), dedicated capacity pool rn-dedicated-us-east-12 serving gpt-3.5-redwood-v1. Observed windows: 09:05–09:55 UTC. This is customer-impacting and on a paid dedicated tenant.

Immediate customer-visible symptoms: increased request latency tails, occasional 504 timeouts,...

title: Historical dashboard panels show stale counts and lose trace links after retention compaction warmup
preview: Issue summary: Several customers (notably LumenHealth) report that historical dashboard panels (time ranges > 30 days) intermittently show zeroed or stale metric counts and their trace links are missing. The problem is transient: panels become correct again after ~10-30 minutes without user action. Impact: dashboard...
text: Issue summary: Several customers (notably LumenHealth) report that historical dashboard panels (time ranges > 30 days) intermittently show zeroed or stale metric counts and their trace links are missing. The problem is transient: panels become correct again after ~10-30 minutes without user action. Impact: dashboards with SLA and usage rollups show incorrect historical values; alerting based on those rollups can under- or over-fire. This affects customer trust in console analytics and can mask billing/usage anomalies. Environment: production us-east region. Affected customers reported on enterprise tier; observed across multiple orgs.

title: Repeated 429 waves after scale-up: HTTP/2 multiplex and token-refill skew on Dedicated pool

text:
Issue summary:
BrightCart began seeing recurring 429 waves across multiple services immediately after a Dedicated pool scale-up event at ~2026-03-10T02:10:00Z. The waves manifest as clustered 429 responses for ~3-8 minutes following host additions and then subside, only to reappear on subsequent scale events. Impact: production customers experienced degraded throughput and failed upload jobs (embeds + short chat requests) for the duration of the waves.

Impact:
- Several background bulk-embed jobs failed and retried concurrently (customer reported increase in error rate from 0.2% -> 4.6%).
- Interactive chat latency spiked and some calls returned 429s, affecting UI experience for a subset of users (approx 3-5% of active sessions).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (customer-owned reservation) in prod-us-east (us-east-1)
- API access over multiplexed HTTP/2 (gRPC-web) via customer edge pr...

title: Delayed TTFB and 504s for long-sequence completions caused by ALB keepalive churn + proxy buffering
preview:

Customers running long-generation requests (2000+ token completions) intermittently see very large time-to-first-byte (TTFB) or final 504s despite backend logs indicating model started generating. Impact: several enterprise customers on Dedicated experienced increased error rates and perceived perform...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers running long-generation requests (2000+ token completions) intermittently see very large time-to-first-byte (TTFB) or final 504s despite backend logs indicating model started generating. Impact: several enterprise customers on Dedicated experienced increased error rates and perceived performance regressions since 2026-03-07.

Impact:
- LexaDocs reported ~8% error rate on long completions; user-facing slow requests and failed job retries.
- Symptom pattern: request accepted, no bytes for 8–30s, then ALB or gateway returns 504 or connection reset. In other cases first bytes arrive but final completion truncated.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (us-east-1) behind ALB -> nginx sidecar -> serving runtime.
- Large tracing headers and cookie payloads (custom CSRF + jwt rotation) present for affected customers.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Send a streaming genera...

title: Service account impersonation scoping prevents delegated admin operations in private prod
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that new service account tokens cannot perform delegated admin ops (user management and billing read) after migrating to a private deployment token broker. Impact: Multiple automation jobs and SRE runbooks fail; customer-facing admin UI returns 403 when acting-as a service account. En...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports that new service account tokens cannot perform delegated admin ops (user management and billing read) after migrating to a private deployment token broker. Impact: Multiple automation jobs and SRE runbooks fail; customer-facing admin UI returns 403 when acting-as a service account. Environment: Private deployment (Aurora private VPC), prod - us-east, Redwood private control plane with identity-proxy v1.9.2 and token-broker v0.8.2. Observed start: 2026-03-09 18:20 UTC. Business impact: Blocks Aurora's automated onboarding and scheduled invoice exports; they are a HIPAA-regulated customer and cannot proceed with several tasks until impersonation works.

text:
Issue summary:
Sustained degradation in tokens/sec and increased 5xx/timeouts for tenant 'AcmeAI' on their dedicated pool (pool-id: pool-usw-41). Starts ~2026-03-10 02:15 UTC and persisted despite autoscaler activity.

Impact:

- Components engaged: dedicated-pool, autoscaler, scheduler, serving-runtime, api-gateway

title: Streaming session stalls then emits duplicated partial tokens after keepalive refill behind CDN
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer Fetchfolio reports that long-running streaming responses (SSE and WS) intermittently pause for ~6-12s and then resume with duplicated partial token sequences. This occurs when connections traverse the customer's CloudFront distribution in front of our edge and only for sessions that had keepa...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer Fetchfolio reports that long-running streaming responses (SSE and WS) intermittently pause for ~6-12s and then resume with duplicated partial token sequences. This occurs when connections traverse the customer's CloudFront distribution in front of our edge and only for sessions that had keepalive refresh traffic from the CDN.

Impact:
- Affects production traffic for a single dedicated tenant (Fetchfolio).
- Stalls cause UI timeouts and duplicate partial tokens which confuse the client's incremental-merge logic (app shows duplicated phrases).
- Reproducible ~1-2x / 1000 streams during peak traffic windows.

Environment:
- Customer dedicated deployment routed through CloudFront -> Redwood edge LB -> api-gateway -> streaming-proxy -> serving-runtime.
- Region: us-east.
- Models: redwood-1-large, openlm-13b-v2.

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1. Start long pr...

Issue summary:

title: Service accounts lost Console/CI access after Okta group prefix remapping
preview: Issue summary: Several internal service accounts and a small set of CI pipelines started receiving 403/permission-denied errors when calling the Redwood Console API and internal admin endpoints immediately after an Okta group naming convention change was applied by Identity. Impact: 7 service accounts and 4 CI workf...
text: Issue summary: Several internal service accounts and a small set of CI pipelines started receiving 403/permission-denied errors when calling the Redwood Console API and internal admin endpoints immediately after an Okta group naming convention change was applied by Identity. Impact: 7 service accounts and 4 CI workflows failed auth checks; internal tooling and alerting UIs were inaccessible for owners. Environment: Production identity sync (SCIM) + Redwood Console RBAC mapping, us-east region. Timeline: group prefix remapping applied 2026-03-10 07:15 UTC, first failures observed 07:21 UTC. Expected: group membership mapping should preserve effective RBAC roles for service principals and bot users. Observed: group-based roles no longer resolved for SAML/SCIM-provisioned service accounts because Okta attribute used for mapping changed from groups.name to groups.displayName in the recent...

title: SSO auth proxy header collision causes intermittent 401/403 for analytics gateway login
preview:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail duri...
text:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail during peak minutes (observed 09:00–11:00 PDT).
- Automated ingestion jobs that rely on service-to-service SSO occasionally fail with 401s, triggering retries and delayed dashboards.

Environment:
- Prod (us-west-2) behind identity-proxy fleet (k8s, istio sidecar + custom auth middleware).

Observed behavior:
- Requests that should be allowed by Okta SSO show a 401 from the analytics-gateway.
- When retries succeed, request headers show slightly different casing/order for auth-related headers.
- No broad Okta outage; only services routed through the updated proxy are affected.

title: Post-SSD repair: systemd-resume hang after hibernation prevents fleet enrollment and SCCM check-in on Ubuntu 22.04
preview:
Issue summary:
After SSD replacement and standard depot provisioning, several Ubuntu 22.04 laptops are failing to complete post-repair check-in. The devices appear to boot normally but never reach fleet enrollment (fleet-agent) and SCCM client check-in. Investigations show the machine is stuck in the systemd 'resume...
text:
Issue summary:
After SSD replacement and standard depot provisioning, several Ubuntu 22.04 laptops are failing to complete post-repair check-in. The devices appear to boot normally but never reach fleet enrollment (fleet-agent) and SCCM client check-in. Investigations show the machine is stuck in the systemd 'resume' target after a hibernation/resume cycle triggered by the depot hardware test.

Impact:
- Affected devices are not joining inventory or receiving policy, delaying redeployment to users.
- ~6 devices reported from depot on 2026-03-08 and 2026-03-09.
- Operational impact: rework and manual reprovisioning required for redeployments.

Environment:
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (custom corporate image built via imaging pipeline)
- kernel 5.15.0-80-generic (corp-pinned)
- Fleet-agent v1.8.2, SCCM agent shim for Linux v2.1.0
- SSD vendor: Western Digital SN730 (replacement units from depot ...

title: NetworkManager writes duplicate default routes during imaging causing AAD join timeout in ap-southeast
preview:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS netwo...
text:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS network error) or attempt to use an internal management VPN endpoint, failing domain registration and stalling the task sequence.

Impact:
- ~5-8% of devices in the last 48h fail AAD join during imaging and are left in the 'Autopilot not completed' state.
- Affected devices require manual intervention (local admin join + re-run of post-provision scripts) delaying depot turn-around.
- Mostly impacts APAC staging fleet and a small subset of production depot images.

Environment:
- Imaging orchestrator: SCCM task sequence handing off to WinPE -> OOBE scripts (internal imaging-playb...

title: Clarify provisional license reclaim window and equipment-return grace period for immediate offboarding
preview:
Issue summary:
HR received reports from Finance and IT that when an employee is placed on Immediate Termination (ITerm) in Workday, the seat/license in third-party SaaS (notably the design/legal tool vendor) remains billable for up to 14 days and the assigned corporate laptop sometimes remains tagged as 'active' in ...
text:
Issue summary:
HR received reports from Finance and IT that when an employee is placed on Immediate Termination (ITerm) in Workday, the seat/license in third-party SaaS (notably the design/legal tool vendor) remains billable for up to 14 days and the assigned corporate laptop sometimes remains tagged as 'active' in our asset management inventory. This ticket seeks clarification of the intended policy (grace windows) and the operational mechanics (which systems enforce the reclaim/return windows).

Impact:
- Potential short-term double-billing for SaaS seats when seats are quickly re-assigned.
- Delays in device recovery leading to inventory inaccuracies and security exposure for high-risk departures.
- Confusion between HR, IT, and Finance on expected timelines and required manual steps.

Environment:
- Identity sync: Okta -> SCIM -> downstream SaaS (prod)
- Asset tracking: FleetOps (...

title: Partial-stream resume replay leads to duplicate external webhook side-effects for Python function-calls
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports duplicate external API calls (webhook POSTs) when using Python SDK streaming + function-calling. Behavior occurs after a network interruption causes the client to reconnect and resume a partially delivered streaming response. The resumed session appears to replay the function invocati...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports duplicate external API calls (webhook POSTs) when using Python SDK streaming + function-calling. Behavior occurs after a network interruption causes the client to reconnect and resume a partially delivered streaming response. The resumed session appears to replay the function invocation without the original idempotency token reaching the downstream webhook, resulting in side-effect duplication (duplicate invoices / duplicate task creation).

Impact:
- Customer (SlateCRM Inc.) is observing duplicate third-party webhook side-effects impacting billing and downstream task systems.
- Occurs intermittently on long-running streaming interactions where the client reconnects to resume.
- Business impact: customer flagged as P1 due to operational impact on billing pipeline.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (customer-owned VPC) in us-east region.
- SDK: python-sd...

title: Intermittent p99 timeouts after client stream restarts due to continuous-batcher linger accumulation
preview: Issue summary: Customers (NimbleRetail dedicated tenancy) observed intermittent p99 user-facing timeouts (504 / request timeouts) concentrated after client-side stream restarts. Impact: Multiple production routes serving chat completions saw p95->p99 divergence, with p99 climbing from ~450ms baseline to 3-6s and occ...
text:
Issue summary: Customers (NimbleRetail dedicated tenancy) observed intermittent p99 user-facing timeouts (504 / request timeouts) concentrated after client-side stream restarts. Impact: Multiple production routes serving chat completions saw p95->p99 divergence, with p99 climbing from ~450ms baseline to 3-6s and occasional 504s. Affected customer reports started 2026-03-10 14:20 UTC and continued in waves.

Environment: prod (us-east), dedicated pool tenant for NimbleRetail, models: redwood-1.0-mk4 (default), fallback to gptj-14b configured.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Client opens a streaming connection and makes a short request (~12 tokens).
2) Client intentionally restarts the stream shortly after (~300-800ms) to refresh headers/session.
3) Server continuous-batcher observed many short-lived streams that resume quickly; linger timers accumulate pending micro-batches....

title: Intermittent websocket half-close after corporate TLS rekey -> trailing 'done' envelope never arrives
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent websocket sessions that appear to close from the server side after a TLS rekey performed by their corporate TLS-inspecting proxy. The stream is truncated: the final JSON envelope with status/done metadata is never received, leaving the client waiting indefinitely. Impact:...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent websocket sessions that appear to close from the server side after a TLS rekey performed by their corporate TLS-inspecting proxy. The stream is truncated: the final JSON envelope with status/done metadata is never received, leaving the client waiting indefinitely. Impact: Multiple production users at Acme AI Labs experience truncated generations and must reissue requests; affects business-critical inference pipelines. Observed first at ~2026-03-09 22:15 PST and recurred several times. Environment: prod cluster behind ALB -> CloudFront -> corporate TLS-inspection -> customer client. No code changes on Redwood side around times of first occurrence.

title: Long-lived keepalive connections from small-stream clients holding GPU slots causing regional capacity squeeze
preview:
Issue summary:
BrightForms is reporting intermittent throughput collapse for their dedicated pool in us-east starting 2026-03-10 11:40 UTC. Symptoms align with a regional GPU supply squeeze where many GPUs appear allocated but traffic shows a high proportion of very short inference streams with persistent TCP/TLS ke...
text:
Issue summary:
BrightForms is reporting intermittent throughput collapse for their dedicated pool in us-east starting 2026-03-10 11:40 UTC. Symptoms align with a regional GPU supply squeeze where many GPUs appear allocated but traffic shows a high proportion of very short inference streams with persistent TCP/TLS keepalive.

Impact:
- Customer-facing routes (real-time chat) p95/p99 latency sharply increased; observed request queues and autoscaler unable to reclaim GPUs quickly.
- Other tenants in the same reserved cluster experienced degraded throughput and increased fallbacks to lower-cap models.
- Business impact: customer reported degraded UX for production traffic across multiple endpoints.

Timeline & quick facts:
- 2026-03-10 11:38 UTC: BrightForms first observed degraded latency and opened a support channel.
- 2026-03-10 11:45 UTC: Support identified elevated "active_slot_count...

title: Layered 429s observed during rolling API token swapback causing throughput collapse
preview: Issue summary: Customer (Acme Analytics, dedicated) reported sudden and sustained 429s beginning ~2026-03-10T14:20Z that coincided with a scheduled rolling API key rotation. Impact: interactive chat and batch embed pipelines degraded; sustained 30-60% request rejection for 20 minutes and client-side retry storms. En...
text:
Issue summary: Customer (Acme Analytics, dedicated) reported sudden and sustained 429s beginning ~2026-03-10T14:20Z that coincided with a scheduled rolling API key rotation. Impact: interactive chat and batch embed pipelines degraded; sustained 30-60% request rejection for 20 minutes and client-side retry storms. Environment: prod-us-west (us-west-2), dedicated pool id dp-88. Customer reports that their orchestrator performs aggressive short retries and they observed token swaps in their logs.

Initial hypothesis: rolling credential refresh led to swapback behavior where some ingress proxies temporarily presented rotated keys while backend accounting still counted against the old token windows. This created token-window misalignment and effective fanout of short-lived keys across pooled connections, exhausting tenant burst buckets and triggering layered 429s.

Why ticket created: cust...

title: SDK retry storm + small connection pool causing serialized streaming requests and intermittent inference timeouts
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a recent regression in perceived inference latency and intermittent request timeouts for streaming responses. The customer is on a Dedicated deployment and uses our official Go and Python SDKs behind an internal gateway. Impact: API calls that previously returned streaming tokens with...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a recent regression in perceived inference latency and intermittent request timeouts for streaming responses. The customer is on a Dedicated deployment and uses our official Go and Python SDKs behind an internal gateway. Impact: API calls that previously returned streaming tokens within ~120–200ms now see long tails up to 6–10s or outright 504/ETIMEDOUT for some requests.

Observed behavior:
- Increased end-to-end latency for streaming calls from a subset of customer hosts beginning 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC.
- Spikes of 429/502/504 correlated with client-side retries and connection churn.
- Host-level connection pools show active connections ~= configured max_connections_per_host, while requests queue on the client.

Expected behavior:
- Streaming responses begin delivering tokens within expected latency envelope; client retries/backoff should not serialize...

title: Intermittent console 504 / aborted uploads: gRPC-web upload stream resets behind customer ADC that coalesces HTTP/2 SETTINGS frames
preview: Issue summary: Intermittent upload failures and 504s observed in Redwood Console for a Private deployment. Failures reproduce only when the user's traffic flows through their on-prem ADC which performs HTTP/2/TCP optimizations. Impact: uploads fail to complete, leading to customer inability to persist prompt dataset...
text: Issue summary: Intermittent upload failures and 504s observed in Redwood Console for a Private deployment. Failures reproduce only when the user's traffic flows through their on-prem ADC which performs HTTP/2/TCP optimizations. Impact: uploads fail to complete, leading to customer inability to persist prompt datasets and occasional console timeouts; affects a subset of users behind the ADC, not all tenants.

title: WebSocket multiplexing: unacknowledged mid-stream chunks and missing final token on reconnection
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent WebSocket streaming sessions that appear to resume after a short gap but the conversation misses the final token(s) and client-side shows last chunk unacknowledged. Behavior started after they enabled multiplexed connections and moved workload onto a dedicated pool.

Impa...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent WebSocket streaming sessions that appear to resume after a short gap but the conversation misses the final token(s) and client-side shows last chunk unacknowledged. Behavior started after they enabled multiplexed connections and moved workload onto a dedicated pool.

Impact:
- Several end-user chat sessions lose the final portion of generated messages (often the last sentence) after a 5–30s silent period.
- Client sees socket resume (code 1000) or a short reconnect, but server-side logs show last chunk delivered and not marked acknowledged by the multiplex layer.
- Customer reports impact on UX for long-turn responses and higher risk for partial instructions being delivered.

Environment:
- Customer: ParcelScale (dedicated)
- Region: prod us-east-1 (dedicated pool id dp-87)
- API mode: WebSocket multiplexing enabled, single socket transport...

title: Cohort signal loss during synchronized canary flip caused unpinned routing to quantized variant
preview: Issue summary: During a synchronized canary roll for redwood-v1-large we observed that a small customer cohort lost explicit model pin state and was routed to the quantized variant (redwood-v1-large-q4). Impact: ~1400 requests over a 12m window returned lower-quality outputs and tokenization deltas; customer observe...
text: Issue summary: During a synchronized canary roll for redwood-v1-large we observed that a small customer cohort lost explicit model pin state and was routed to the quantized variant (redwood-v1-large-q4). Impact: ~1400 requests over a 12m window returned lower-quality outputs and tokenization deltas; customer observed increased hallucination in 18% of affected responses (reported by customer and confirmed by telemetry). Environment: Dedicated tenant on prod:us-east-1. Steps to reproduce: 1) Start a weighted canary (10%) for redwood-v1-large across us-east-1 with prewarm enabled. 2) Trigger an orchestrator snapshot merge (scheduled merge job). 3) Send steady tenant traffic that relies on explicit version pin headers. Observed behaviour: A cohort of sessions lost model pin during the snapshot merge and were routed to redwood-v1-large-q4 (fallback path) instead of honoring tenant pin. Tok...

title: Throughput degradation and intermittent request timeouts caused by GPU affinity + batch-size mismatch on dedicated pool
preview: Issue summary: Customer Streamlytic is experiencing sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504/timeout errors for inference requests on their dedicated us-west reservation. The pattern started after a week of burst traffic and a recent autoscaler tuning change.
text: Issue summary: Customer Streamlytic is experiencing sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504/timeout errors for inference requests on their dedicated us-west reservation. The pattern started after a week of burst traffic and a recent autoscaler tuning change.
